creator: tommyturbine@riseup.net
text:
Soundcard in an Acer Aspire 3000 laptop.

/dev/sndstat:

Installed devices:
pcm0: <SiS 7012> (play/rec) default


output of 'mixer':

Mixer vol      is currently set to  88:88
Mixer pcm      is currently set to  76:76
Mixer speaker  is currently set to  75:75
Mixer line     is currently set to  75:75
Mixer mic      is currently set to   0:0
Mixer cd       is currently set to  75:75
Mixer rec      is currently set to  75:75
Mixer igain    is currently set to   0:0
Mixer ogain    is currently set to  50:50
Mixer line1    is currently set to  75:75
Mixer phin     is currently set to   0:0
Mixer phout    is currently set to   0:0
Recording source: vol


mpg123 looks like its playing to the device, but not sound. catting a
text file to /dev/dsp0.0 doesn't make any sound (either with headphones
or with the onboard speakers). A cheap usb soundcard does work with
this machine.

How-To-Repeat: Install FreeBSD 10.0-RC2 on an Acer Aspire 3000. Try to play an audio
file of any description.
